decided_anyway.text: The governor appoints the Secretary of State, who oversees elections, and numerous board and commission members including the Texas Transportation Commission and Texas Parks and Wildlife Commission
The governor has line-item veto power over the state budget, allowing rejection of specific appropriations without vetoing the entire bill
The governor directs state law enforcement priorities through the Texas Department of Public Safety and can deploy the Texas National Guard

turnout.summary: 11,291,854 votes cast; under-30 voter data not available in retrieved sources for 2024

decided_anyway.text: U.S. Senators vote to confirm or reject federal judicial appointments, including Supreme Court justices, with lifetime tenure implications regardless of voter turnout
U.S. Senators vote on federal budget appropriations and tax legislation that determines spending on defense, healthcare, and infrastructure without requiring voter participation
U.S. Senators ratify treaties and authorize use of military force, committing the nation to international obligations and armed conflict regardless of electoral turnout
